Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Heightened anxiety and hypervigilance related to a workplace whistleblower situation; worries about retaliation and job security, with intermittent insomnia and difficulty concentrating.

Physical therapy self-management consult with a colleague for flare-up of low back pain (Feb 2026)
Telehealth visit for sleep problems and stress (Feb 2026)

Purchase of a personal laptop and external encrypted drive for document organization
Co-pay for primary care visit and lab work

social.social_media_activity: Low activity; maintains a private Instagram and a LinkedIn profile for professional networking. Recently reduced posting and tightened privacy settings to avoid workplace monitoring.

Concerns about retaliation, being labeled a troublemaker, or being pushed out

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 8:00am–4:30pm, with two late evenings per month for documentation catch-up; occasional Saturday coverage once every 6–8 weeks.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure self-check at a local pharmacy kiosk every Sunday morning
Monthly primary care follow-up (temporarily, until BP stabilizes)

Friend’s birthday dinner in Federal Hill (Mar 2026)
schedule.daily_routine: Early wake-up for stretching and a short walk; clinic hours with heavy documentation; avoids discussing work details at home until late evening; tries to wind down with reading or quiet TV but often reviews notes related to the ethical issue before bed.

professional.company: Outpatient orthopedic physical therapy clinic affiliated with a regional healthcare network (Baltimore-area)
projects: Implementing a standardized outcomes tracking workflow (FOTO/functional measures) for the clinic
Developing a patient education handout series on low back pain and return-to-work conditioning
Mentoring a new graduate PT on documentation and plan-of-care updates
professional.performance_issues: Increased stress has led to slower documentation turnaround and occasional missed internal deadlines; he is also deliberately limiting access to certain records and communications due to fear of retaliation, which has been noticed by a supervisor.
workplace_relationships: Tense dynamic with clinic director after Tao questioned unusually high visit frequency recommendations
Uneasy relationship with billing lead who pressures clinicians to code higher complexity and extend plans of care
Supportive but cautious rapport with one senior PT who advises him to protect his license and document concerns

hobbies: Walking the Inner Harbor and Patterson Park trails
Cooking Indian and Mediterranean meals at home
Reading nonfiction (health policy and investigative journalism)
Playing pickup badminton at a local community center
habits: Tracks blood pressure at home and logs readings
Limits caffeine after noon due to hypertension and sleep issues
Uses a password manager and enabled two-factor authentication after workplace concerns escalated
Keeps a private, time-stamped journal of workplace incidents and his own observations
Occasional late-night snacking during high-stress weeks
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in the Canton area with his partner; keeps a small locked file box at home for personal documents and avoids storing workplace-related notes on shared devices due to fear of retaliation.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked, moderately low-sodium diet for blood pressure; prioritizes lean protein and vegetables but struggles with consistency during stressful periods.
